textByHash.c30e4025ca15ad10a44919415c47d33862dbeb53: Well we need to go help!
textByHash.932d42a73bfcce9f148fdaae7986ab735a5a8ac4: Damn them vampyres. We need to find a way to turn things in our favour.
textByHash.abef201d6679a1f1d8ec5a5eacddd95e5a6d08ee: What do you need it for?
textByHash.414e620385f1fd849d4578a16d16bc7866f35f92: Well I can hardly say no to that. Here you go.
textByHash.b5decdaaf5e890b823feb355ff2516af078c31ac: Well I'd be happy to help but you don't have enough room for it.
textByHash.2386c1e50625c11627dfe7a35218e9740501dcd7: Looks like you have one right there.
textByHash.c8ec1d7f62acc05ab4b76d75ee72ec8634a10353: But I already gave it to you.
textByHash.cbf2e8046b2b4312c85e46ec1e6474a9b337e60e: Well you'll just have to make another then. That was the only one I had.
textByHash.c423ed14360ff95cea72e1b10992b86612afbe2b: Well I'd say our mission to establish a new hideout has been a great success. All thanks to you, of course.
textByHash.a99a21172296aa0ae222a287b3bf44045c3174ef: Very good. I'd say our mission to establish a new hideout has been a great success. All thanks to you, of course.
textByHash.86cb40fbd12a95c3818cad703078352752b3f8a9: With the hideout sorted, we can now start focusing on the vampyres again. We're looking to re-establish contact with the Sanguinesti region.
textByHash.fffd9bdc43b40f4a8f7b804e56d5f5bcb47d6dd9: I also want to look into a diplomatic mission into Misthalin. The support of the king would be very helpful in our fight against Drakan.
textByHash.4ba50d2a61e99a8c2242a80298fdebcd608ed089: Well, we could use some help with scouting a route into the Sanguinesti region. Are you interested in learning more details?
textByHash.50804f8a5612e3e608dc036634eb54c6034c699c: There should be another group of Myreque working in the Sanguinesti region. We need to re-establish contact with them. To do that, we need a route into the region.
textByHash.97148cc4f5788cedf197fd46a774b4443ab870c5: The entire region is surrounded by a wall that you'll find impossible to get over.
textByHash.efd6e05818da7ee9fdd75a4905c195035863eec0: That said, you might be able to find a way around the wall at the point where it meets the sea. Perhaps there's something in Burgh de Rott that could help you.
textByHash.8959d8d82d670590c6655e461c08d9bc12bbcd1b: Once you're in, try and establish contact with the Myreque there. I'm sure they'll have sympathisers amongst the citizens. Someone should be able to point you in the right direction.
textByHash.cd1cd557ccd59dcceeb737ae09b27d54710982e3: One last thing to be aware of is the vyrewatch. They're powerful vampyres that patrol the entire region. We haven't found any way of killing them. Even the Rod of Ivandis does nothing!
textByHash.babe6e661e7588fb7afbccb1d77efa64d77e0dea: It's a large region to the east of here. The southern part is a city called Meiyerditch. Humans live there in ghettos and are regularly tithed by the vampyres.
textByHash.8d523153201542982f97b1869cf858464bf4c32e: The northern part is the vampyre capital of Darkmeyer. It is from his castle there that Lowerniel Drakan rules over all of Morytania.
textByHash.7e40532f9cc87c6c0187306711ef2beff2a64b3f: Certainly. The Myreque, or at least the resistance that has now become the Myreque, originated in the region. Previously there was nothing.
textByHash.9f595771f6ad84d5f6b9b3d32e70adb770d8fdaa: It was many years ago that a small group, led by a man called Safalaan Hallow, managed to escape from the ghettos of Meiyerditch.
textByHash.ef0ef1f765ce6eba66f0556e8df761832aef7483: They got information out about what was happening and helped to organise the resistance, which today is called the Myreque.
textByHash.2f619477eaa8725ce13ddb9cf5b3960d88593560: The name was given to us by the locals, who named us after the place where we hid in Mort Myre.
textByHash.d925a4468534023788ee27666de83469296a65fd: Very well. We'll be here if you change your mind.
textByHash.5a4a9d348294177f422f9a8e70c515f1a066d323: Oh, the usual. Keep a low profile and keep an eye on what the vampyres are up to. There's also plenty of research to do with all the new information you've found for us.
textByHash.58bc8219bf91b548a2a1b4ffdfffefb50f3141ac: The region is where the heaviest blood tithing takes place. It's also home to some of the more senior members of vampyre society, including Lowerniel Drakan himself.
textByHash.b96f3676cc91b2936d1dbaff6a76679642e8daf3: Assuming the vampyres haven't put an end to them, there should be another group of Myreque operating in the region. We will need their support in the fight against the vampyres.
textByHash.cbe31decb499d26ebc459f04023f254f4260172a: We can't hope to fight the power of the vampyres alone. We'll need help from Misthalin by the bucket-load if we're to stand a chance against them.
textByHash.cd23685f2ddcb853bfe9e3e5879d993358282077: We need you to find a way into the Sanguinesti region. Once you're in, you need to try and make contact with any members of the Sanguinesti order of the Myreque.
textByHash.c446b6b8b496665e40bf4c389ebf6a48a2382efa: Good news. Presumably you took precautions not to be followed?
textByHash.ac67c4e77e89de9a3d5e21ccff3ea1cb2bff24a5: Now that you've made your way into the Sanguinesti region, we need you to try and make contact with the Myreque there.
textByHash.a2375d2563542d4a7c27b06c0ffd75941a77cd8f: You may need to ask some of the citizens, but try to be discreet! Remember to avoid the vyrewatch at all costs!
textByHash.2f4e7cad15e437dd2fad4b856c6d458115b15d53: You may get lost while in the city. It might be worth your while to invest in some teleportation devices to get yourself out.
textByHash.f472359c8b140043a365af7a17f3be79adbf418e: Well, please bring it to me when you can. I'd be very interested to hear what they have to say.
textByHash.b75dc81812d93587d65676e69ed24617dceba62c: Great! Please let me see it.
textByHash.18630046a37247a98e03264a891bd8d314a9b07c: Hmm, sounds like they're struggling just as much as we are. At least they still live though.
textByHash.ae3133ebb835a4a38527fe528a5f5027903fc837: I need some time to consider how we can best support the Sanguinesti Myreque. In the meantime, I have a new task for you.
textByHash.61ad0df79a8430ccd50160612aece8cb4748c93b: I've had word from Drezel that there's something strange going on at Paterdomus. Head up there and see what support you can offer.
textByHash.ba33920429d30f4b42b89986580b6f415257f7bd: Once you're done, press onwards to Varrock and seek an audience with King Roald. Express the desperate nature of the situation here and beg him to send troops.
textByHash.9fd1cff573d23cf1e3bfcfdb6b8342bc5fa5eec0: The vampyres must be behind this! Did you speak with Roald yet? We need his support if we are to end this suffering.
textByHash.ad1add5ae944ae44a6ea29659475b4fff563ac3d: It's good of you to return with this information, but we need to know Roald's answer before we can make any further plans.
textByHash.df3ebd20d8e65d770982475426b7a77b0afce917: Guthixian edicts? How do they prevent Roald from sending troops?
textByHash.0c1d7b109d0de33df33e92a77fc4c815e3b04397: There has long been a desire by the Church of Saradomin to see Guthixian power eroded away. This stinks of politics. They're just using the situation here for their own gain!
textByHash.1d214eaa79a9374cbba616b6de0d9def283cea33: Well that's something I guess. Not exactly what we were hoping for though.
textByHash.5a11481371e29ca2665ecdfb3d3a332bc7e41a14: We must inform the Sanguinesti order of the Myreque of these events. Please go as fast as you can back to the Meiyerditch hideout and forward this information to them.
textByHash.ce20fc7f3cee602af3e7de6cd0f97a84ebcd5d86: We must inform the Sanguinesti order of the Myreque of what we have learnt. Please go as fast as you can back to the Meiyerditch hideout and forward this information to them.
textByHash.8f1dfdad0be9e43070388b6dbe22cf0fbb8af1e3: Good. It seems like you're really proving your worth. Keep it up.
textByHash.ff1c340db26e28def69a1df347d6485862143165: Well met. It's good to see you again.
textByHash.dde4f8c2e4fb353b251bd59671aa010d79c0bbbc: Well, that's not particularly good is it? You'll have to go back and ask him if he can rewrite the message for you.
textByHash.b29bea3d3beeca69de264a72670dae11fdc59914: Really? Well, let's see it then!
textByHash.a11dee1e776cf5a8b322d7a7f75560f7e7f993ce: Well, it seems that you have more than proven yourself worthy. Safalaan himself has recommended you for a promotion to the rank of a private within the Myreque!
textByHash.f15c227c447634633248f7809328853d484e5ef9: You've done well. I'd say you've earnt a break. Before you go though, here's a little something to thank you for your hard work.
textByHash.cd3656acb3c1c768ef9c96a9b19f769d3bb5918c: Hmm, okay, let's see if I have another one for you... Ah, here we go!
textByHash.0b35b3b44dcfe73b2d322a06fef62f593dcf2977: There's plenty of options for us to move forwards. We'll still keep up intelligence-gathering operations within the local area. We'll also keep looking for weaknesses in the vampyres' defences.
textByHash.e0502492629da3241f41ba7ddd2aebd70f072ca5: I'll probably have to meet up with Safalaan at some point, so we can discuss strategies. This war is far from over. There's plenty of time to contribute so don't worry about that.
textByHash.cd1e0dfb4234216e8b10e86fd044d74330016358: Don't worry, we will. Myreque prevail.
textByHash.1b3c3a6db6a2fe3392ca860228398accdcfed087: You're back. Any luck in Canifis?
textByHash.fc15b655c8200fcf6d4fcae8d687a6f826bf0f26: I feared as much. Worry not though. The day will come when we get our revenge on him and the rest of his kind.
textByHash.af57e8ee1f02132898fb13b3479c271f47f5a318: Anyway, now that you're back, there was something I was hoping you'd help us with.
textByHash.df7e8c6ee5ba5166fcde684f393f2e075e5a15ee: Now that our base of operations is no longer safe, we're going to need to relocate before the vyrewatch descend on us.
textByHash.a61d1a4eb125036be514c3de1aa325d8cb1288ca: To the south of Mort'ton there's an old town known as Burgh de Rott. The place was abandoned but I hear some escapees from Meiyerditch have made their home there.
textByHash.74b0f9d807edc20897b8aa0156ef00c279572dea: Exactly. Head down to Burgh de Rott and see if you can find a suitable place for our new hideout. While you're there, do what you can to forge a good relationship with the townspeople.
textByHash.6e100902bb06a10aadb78bc467a0cc456970f27f: Be sure to take a weapon of silver with you. You'll find it of great use if vampyres attack. Good luck to you.
textByHash.70e1015f624d1d2473d3d5173d7e9b4da195a87f: Very well. You know where we are if you change your mind.
textByHash.511e6a58daa8723f5626e1ebbbd573b4805a70e4: Head to Burgh de Rott and see if the town has a suitable location for our new hideout. While you're there, do what you can to forge a good relationship with the townspeople.
textByHash.6d4e1cae4800fcea6dbfb2bc7f1842f9a7066074: You'll find the town just south of Mort'ton. Good luck.
textByHash.1cd88b1ea69b37a49ade651444fc5e1b8750a17c: Fear not my friend! I will come to your aid!
textByHash.afb234f48e9f260fc1b5249ed36eb30d4c9815d0: Well met, friend. Glad you made it back in one piece.
textByHash.c98475ad19fb8f1697510cacf3edf9cf141d3f07: Ah, excellent. Well Drezel is an old friend and he's agreed to do us a favour. Ivan is too young and inexperienced to be out here with us. He also aspires to be a priest himself.
textByHash.d051103a3f4e1f96780839a78540d6bb095a8dc6: Drezel has agreed to take him in and train him. Once he is older and wiser, he can rejoin our fight as a spiritual advisor.
textByHash.f24e397cae2bed1d2373e1d1653beaa8d8e01e52: Myself, Polmafi and Radigad will soon be heading down to Burgh de Rott. While we do so, I'd appreciate it if you could escort Ivan to Paterdomus.
textByHash.132e1aca565927d67510fd550bc219c838f13adc: Thank you. There's something else I could use your help with as well.
textByHash.6634ed7ef91a95dba16a0d5f987b069fd627c8fb: I'm sure you've noticed by now that our weapons have limited use against the vampyres. Even weapons made of silver only go so far. If we're to have any hope of beating their kind, we'll need to address this problem.
textByHash.1e36cd14eaa561ec9ca7e0ea5a53d47efa6d5d25: If you've met Drezel, then I've no doubt that he's told you the tale of the Seven Priestly Warriors who blessed the Salve and saved Misthalin.
textByHash.ad03d030c929624c2e5f9b551b1c927feaedfadf: Well I have heard rumour that one of these warriors, a man called Ivandis Seergaze, used a weapon of great power against the vampyres. I have also heard that he isn't actually buried at Paterdomus with the others.
textByHash.4b8686777a7c2696c2d6b2b8ce7c8113d2523192: If you could, my friend, you would have my gratitude. Drezel will likely know more, though I find he isn't always forthcoming where rumours are involved.
textByHash.cc6c05521b89231a817b63331f77957dbc33718b: Before you go, do you mind telling Polmafi and Radigad that it's time for us to depart.
textByHash.2bea069cf0149b86725c744a400badd7df249c78: Well met. Do you need something?
textByHash.e126af4875eea3c9e89e59107b50881a6539a605: First, let Polmafi and Radigad know that it's time for us to depart. After you've done that, please could you escort Ivan to Paterdomus.
textByHash.94766455893e6efcb4a831de4fcd047e6300aa52: Once you're there, Drezel might be able to help you with this Ivandis business.
textByHash.41afe3e490b51de8440bde52ca604a396f6f4220: Please could you escort Ivan to Paterdomus. Once you're there, Drezel might be able to help you with this Ivandis business.
textByHash.a7a97d69567e0d1db32dfe12ac97d051b9bfb24e: It could be a load of rubbish, but it may be worth looking into. I recognise at least two of the items he mentioned as ingredients for a potion.
textByHash.7e1c989c42bfb673e4b04f6a5539ca2d35800eb6: The mention of garlic and silver is interesting though. We know vampyres are weak to both. The way he talked though suggests they could be used in a potion as well.
textByHash.b453cc7603cde74588535713da7b519984a1d437: A good question my friend, but I have no answer for you. Perhaps you could experiment?
textByHash.9573c409af24498447505042f0ca9b9b1c84c9f7: He'll be safer there under the eye of Drezel. Drezel can help him further his spiritual skills so that when he is older and wiser, he can rejoin the fight.
textByHash.9850e3a6b87c773736c88b1dc2d010afaa831c4e: Long ago, the Seven Priestly Warriors drove the vampyre forces out of Misthalin and blessed the Salve so that they'd never be able to return.
textByHash.31ef5a9a8b33ec809d4128abf9556f92cf9c8213: Now, the priests of Saradomin would have you believe that all seven are buried beneath Paterdomus. However, I have heard rumour that one of the warriors, Ivandis Seergaze, is actually buried elsewhere.
textByHash.93fe166a540004cfc5a4efa156522a938be9b4c1: He supposedly led countless charges into Morytania and ultimately perished somewhere within these lands. If that is the case, his followers would likely have buried him near the place he died.
textByHash.dacd97b25b219edcdba2357f69322f4655f0600a: The reason this is important is because it is said that Ivandis wielded a weapon of great power against the vampyres. If he died in Morytania, this weapon may have been buried with him.
textByHash.f0921964278d4f88a48aa9805b92265bfc99463b: Thanks for getting Ivan to Paterdomus safely. Let me know if you have any luck finding information on Ivandis.
textByHash.d7be0c5ce9054b5b9fc4070d66714a75271948fe: Well that can only be good news. I imagine it will contain all sorts of useful information. Do let me know if you find anything in there of note.
textByHash.0da7b52dcb3bdab4fc79cd730f62a86f67ebec98: Well if I knew where it was, I'd be there now.
textByHash.4f56dee604719ecafc60e261eac4c09ad70d8dce: I suspect he is buried somewhere in Morytania. He supposedly led countless charges into these lands and ultimately perished while doing so.
textByHash.25a3703360586c372616818e07d47df085c493f6: As for where in Morytania, I can't imagine he'd have made it too far from Paterdomus.
textByHash.56821e7964764a4f9209a6ae220915d474f2e7d1: Ivandis Seergaze was one of the Seven Priestly Warriors. Along with the other six, he managed to hold back the vampyres when they attempted to invade Misthalin.
textByHash.fdd723051c96feec1c2540a3173a5fd3b5811242: The seven of them were also responsible for blessing the Salve, preventing the vampyres from ever crossing.
textByHash.ab9e31192f7769a588637f0342a1b2674ddcbd4d: It is said that Ivandis wielded a great weapon against the vampyres. One that could render them immobile for a short time.
textByHash.f05c862ab2e44ac60062cbc9917bacbd2e3352dd: There are also stories that credit him with the restoring of something called 'Guthix balance'.
textByHash.c895e68bded52133f65d82754381c7bb2d8b3d82: Indeed. As the god of balance, it is said that Guthix will always try to ensure that both Saradomin and Zamorak do not gain an upper hand against the other.
textByHash.29e861c2cb6f44b046968f62d3bd6108ee584e09: Now, why a Saradominist warrior-priest is connected to him, I couldn't say.
textByHash.cebbe50acb2b7930e522d85d3e9d8bdf722ab013: That's great news! Hopefully you can find something of use in there.
textByHash.680b51d3ed61b383fd470a2aff8aff622470b667: A rod you say? This sounds like it could be the weapon we seek.
textByHash.492ac67797f970f411dd2f21d201a8cbce98678b: I hope you are wrong about that. If we cannot break the mould of our historical defeats, we will never be able to stop Drakan.
textByHash.bd18efbcd515449e8a5316128bc31c978485f576: Excellent work. Let me know how it goes.
textByHash.1577985ab3095407c532ff8bc0ef561ef94e2bb0: I do not, I'm afraid. You mentioned that the original rod is still in Ivandis' tomb though. I'm sure studying it will give you a clue as to how it was made.
textByHash.4d58c406c813355c0e7977c9d8226be716c4e761: If that doesn't work, you might be able to learn something at Paterdomus.
textByHash.c5b85146fe7bf66a988168a814d991baf76cb6f8: I believe this rod is the weapon that Ivandis used against the vampyres. The stories say that he used to render them immobile for a short time. You might be able to learn more at Paterdomus.
textByHash.71f777f868e4d653c3b224ef1d1f3f5c29d78945: Hmm, well it certainly looks very authentic. But there doesn't seem to be any enchantment on it, either mortal or devine. Perhaps there is a step of the process you have not completed yet?
textByHash.c38355d9cb92f5b87d677972c79c659377291c71: If the truth is anywhere, I bet it's at Paterdomus.
textByHash.56593530f9cefe092ae2d3377f671c02316621cc: Hmm, well it certainly looks very authentic, and I can see it's been enchanted. But there doesn't seem to be any divine blessing on the item. Perhaps there is a step of the process you have not completed yet?
textByHash.e0c63fad1c69862b6d5eea8ecda0d739ca31a9a2: Hmmm, I can't take it off your hands while you're still using it. If you want to offer it to me, you'll need to unequip it.
textByHash.0fb9bde87caa2b4a9a245e568cc7f0e8b036a9f8: I... I don't believe it! You've really done it!
textByHash.5957a085ec3a2dd580122bd8ef616dd79977d1c8: Well , you have certainly excelled yourself in this task. I believe you may soon be true Myreque material.
textByHash.0d6779e230f5b719ad86be4bbe0eb545ac958498: You... I saw you come in. Harold didn't kill you though so I'm guessing you're no enemy. Who are you?
textByHash.36cff14213f55c037f4d818927621bdf1437fea8: It's true that we could do with some supplies. Thank you for bringing them here. I'll have a proper look over them in a moment but there's something I must finish first.
textByHash.b49051ca3b494cf8c6ad911013e83c8790c637de: Feel free to introduce yourself to the others in the meantime.
textByHash.5409ba2ce54fbfdd65f71480901e814e41cd4528: Ah, well met. We'll discuss those weapons shortly. I need to finish something first though. Feel free to introduce yourself to the others in the meantime.
textByHash.dfb587505bfe5b7ad48274ec09c68e90ece249ed: Well met. I see you've introduced yourself to everyone. Shall we discuss those weapons now? Or was there something else you wanted to talk about?
textByHash.720490fc09d84dba8c09e14d7ae0ded56e8eb0ff: Let's see what you have for us.
textByHash.92f265379358a2dbda3517c17e3e67ef435c00ff: These weapons look great! Many thanks.
textByHash.847a22c14df96fccafd34e016c8928380e6b2170: Ah. I'm afraid you don't have suitable weapons for us. Sorry friend.
textByHash.4e0ff9c7bc1cafb4e035351006e60505078d035d: Well we all have our own specialisms. We could do with a longsword, two shortswords, a dagger, a mace and a warhammer, all made from steel.
textByHash.75bf7b5aaf6723cbba4a30994ad32672586421c2: That's right. I can't say I particularly asked for it. The others look up to me though, so they decided I was the best man for the job.
textByHash.96672a4ae5ab57d9f48dfb0328bcd7e0fcfb85e8: Well I wouldn't say we're an organisation. We're really not that organised.
textByHash.a7dfe75f4d26eaf225cfd9b6afed9c7a7ce84f65: Anyway though, we're a group of Myreque freedom fighters. We're dedicated to mounting a resistance against the Drakan family and their vampyre kin.
textByHash.f40b49995dc98529877bef333d571845b3778798: If legend is to be believed, it was once a beautiful place. But then Lowerniel Drakan and his vampyres arrived.
textByHash.6cd4aaace470c634cdde28ac6b96c96b64bd01da: Lowerniel Vergidiyad Drakan is his full name. He's the vampyre that heads up the Drakan family. Helped by his brother Ranis and his sister Vanescula, he rules over all of Morytania.
textByHash.b944be9ade52871a03fb896c820c200d9e78f986: So would you if you lost everyone dear to you at the hands of such monsters.
textByHash.19d1a90cb587bff59d4841c8a74b147df653ca34: At the moment we're maintaining a low profile here at our hideout. We were recently spotted while out on a scouting mission so we're playing it safe and lying low.
textByHash.dec62619f41c2c09d8db8d4d212a5d9a691487a1: Looking further ahead, we need to build more support if we have any hope of taking on the vampyres. We also need to learn more about their weaknesses, so we can take advantage of them.
textByHash.3dde23cd41b6f6efe4510d2ccd3d7cc1ad05ffb3: Sani... Harold... That murderer killed them!
textByHash.567f1be3106604d1f88b26a6cfe8ebacd3854dd5: I don't understand though. He said you led him here. How?
textByHash.5ba88ae109a08db2c68a40babdb59f1acd07858f: I'm sorry friend, but he clearly took advantage of you. Vampyres can shapeshift to look like humans. Someone from around here might have realised. You were not to know though.
textByHash.a6e98212e99ceacb62a7e77e29c822c4144a08c3: The burden of what happened here is for your conscience to bear. That said, you managed to kill that pet of his. We might all be dead if not for you.
textByHash.d694c884857aa3f43442d7593ccdf4a1bb2d76f0: I imagine you now see why we fight for our freedom.
textByHash.ded041743146a632628d39b4c505cb8bc33f8453: I suspect he will have moved on now. You're welcome to try though. I know a shortcut that you might find useful.
textByHash.832ea3b653bbccd938329c98e2e4ed6fa622029c: If you head back into the main cave and go north you'll reach a wall. There's a hidden door in that wall that leads right into the basement of the Hair of the Dog Tavern.
textByHash.2d0d358fff981971f9e4b70d4b70b2e1b8d7a820: Once you are done, I'd appreciate you coming back here to discuss a few things. You held your own against that creature. We could use someone like you.
textByHash.2dd152427d978ffaf8b80a4ae4a34c81afc84aee: Well we could definitely use someone like you. You should go and see if Vanstrom is still in Canifis though. Come back once you're done, and we'll talk.
textByHash.c7de3bff0591e0bf15430ca8c72ec507ffe373cf: We won't be able to stay here now that Vanstrom knows where we've been hiding. We have some time though. He'll expect that we're all dead. We'll use that time to form a plan of action.
textByHash.403a7517fcc0e7fbfb94274d936f5b87e0394ad1: Well met friend. I'm here on an investigation. There's something strange going on in this town, and I suspect the vampyres are involved.
textByHash.c3802727bf29f69bcad3fa9d4a3206c88cc0756f: Not at the moment. Keep checking in though, I may have need of you soon.
textByHash.7720f7b324bebd251d575b2161c4e2b04f3e28c0: Well an extra pair of hands is always beneficial. If you have the time of course?
textByHash.049c8167c8557d5cc008b317add8953714834736: This town has recently become home to an illness known as the Sleeper Plague. It's a nasty affliction. Those infected fall into a deep sleep, never to wake.
textByHash.8cc3bde93e7d56a326b4976849862ebae4de9090: Now, Morytania is no stranger to odd afflictions. However, I've been doing some digging. Apparently, this isn't the first time this illness has beset Slepe. In fact, they say it's how the town was named.
textByHash.283bfa8feff57330c1e28c3df84a1bf0dcb0f51d: I do. The first victim of the new Sleeper Plague was none other than Lady Crombwick. She rules over this town from Crombwick Manor, along with her husband.
textByHash.60056bc2016f21465bbad0474d5d26c036f4f4d4: He's not been seen since she fell ill, and their manor has been locked down.
textByHash.b5b0be7fa3c858f1b73b2c3c9ad187cc57212807: Here's the interesting part though. Vampyres have been seen around the manor. Apparently they showed up around the time when the Sleeper Plague returned.
textByHash.12d506dc9d5aef49b767b053265e4591dc967010: I do. The question is, why?
textByHash.92891a07db444fa48b33c1d683a6f0f3a2cbd969: We'll split up. I'm going to try and learn more about these vampyres at the manor. While I do that, I want you to try and find the source of the illness.
textByHash.4b7b4194b8ad5b38dab39afeb2fe30cb10f9c570: They're looking after the victims in this church here. I'd recommend you make that your starting point.
textByHash.86094c80ada060c7e0705b82c7797a1d8a4bbd8c: We haven't been doing too bad. Even out in Mort Myre, the impact of Ranis' death can be seen.
textByHash.d31d7c40ac8b542daa650f3cc15c0edf913558b3: People have a little bit more hope now, and it has become easier for us to gather support.
textByHash.edf649b275d6c9e7e323d9a509828cb1f5aaef29: Of course, there are downsides. The vampyres are hunting us with ever greater determination. But this is the life we chose.
textByHash.fba721aec7a6512a4fccdc4f5970f73df2e45ce0: Slepe? It's wretched. The people of this town follow the teachings of Zamorak. Some are no better than the vampyres themselves.
textByHash.4a4782fdb36cc7bc1f713e12fced2ff61fc0f3aa: As well as any humans can. All settlements in Morytania must pay the vampyres in blood tithes, but the people of Slepe do so willingly.
textByHash.cb2e7c6ecd050c62fba4e018c7d8002319e02376: They see the vampyres as fellow servants of Zamorak. Of course, the vampyres only really serve themselves.
textByHash.3cb55f43291968b6c2369a27e5e07e2b6409050a: Apparently this place was founded by a group called the Sisterhood, former healers of Saradomin that turned to Zamorak.
textByHash.e689851378a2f02f43ca901a13ad1d37b029d37d: Their order still exists to this day. You'll probably see a few of them around the town.
textByHash.d7e58a736015caa3b718353599ebc672091397ad: What I told you is pretty much what I know. The illness causes people to fall to sleep, never to wake again.
textByHash.318ad9cf33d58e5852e3662c4e1e1f837f4d74ee: There's a rumour that the infected are also granted immortality, though I don't know if there's any truth in this.
textByHash.f1980db2a7a367e1c350dfc0ea0f7d9ab8321db0: Indeed. If history is to be believed, this is the second time the Sleeper Plague has hit Slepe. The first occurrence was many years ago and is supposedly what gave the town its name.
textByHash.0dadcde0690398a4de18797dea92b75e9b2232ab: That's all I know. That said, I suspect you might be able to learn more if you do some digging around town.
textByHash.e6145f5590f5f64aa3c726915e19ed04fedcb52c: You need to try and find the source of the Sleeper Plague. I'd start in the church. That's where they're looking after the victims.
textByHash.8a701fd5c81a3d979b57d74d07d4e001bc06b7ae: Well met friend. How goes the investigation?
textByHash.b33abd008c3d8a7b202811b558f7737ee976ddd8: Interesting. Might be worth a look.
textByHash.e8cdf90eb130a8bc15df4176d6c057927750828a: Well met friend. Anything to report?
textByHash.112da75ff1c85b880680e3f9e1d3870ff42ab7e4: Excellent work friend. This should stop any new cases of the illness.
textByHash.d8f1fc965333cf3dde38d49e3293e947154069a7: The vampyre you speak of is a noble called Damien Leucurte. He's a known quantity to us. My investigations at the manor also involved the two of them, but let's hear the rest of your findings first.
textByHash.f1b53cfd5286dcc3a6531bebb6c4556017d9e81e: This is all very interesting. The citizens of this town are Zamorakians and willingly give their blood to the vampyres. Why would Damien need the Sleeper Plague?
textByHash.aea5063c976d3869abc25c581182f7d4b529948e: That would make sense. I've heard rumours that the vampyre population has grown such that demand for blood now exceeds supply. Damien may be looking for ways to satisfy this demand.
textByHash.f0d21ee82d5cf8fb949b3b1f85a68e43bbfe990b: Even so, this seems extreme. And to go behind Vanstrom's back as well? Maybe their overcrowding issues are bigger than we first realised.
textByHash.14de6ffb562b7851987e6f1dd6e33ac8053ccf51: I have an idea about that, although my information is not quite as exciting as yours I'm afraid.
textByHash.2bc91ad5094a85f65a0ce49ab7df4ff3c030e2b2: While I was investigating the manor, I discovered that it has indeed been taken over by vampyres. Our friend Damien seems to be the leader of this group.
textByHash.7a8c95f8e15dae67891f76e7abd66f7646efb111: During my time there, I also managed to spy on Vanstrom and Damien. I only heard a small part of their conversation, but Vanstrom didn't seem very happy, which matches up with what you heard.
textByHash.8d213e723294e120f07d6e7b69b117598ec6f180: At one point, Vanstrom insulted Damien for his recklessness and accused him of demanding too much from the werewolves. Sounds like they've also been gathering extra blood without permission.
textByHash.db508518610e6b8a6f53c53e49ef2b28d6cea62a: Yes, that encounter also sprung into my mind. It sounds like things are even worse now.
textByHash.3f9ce2a1bd3d319b87948b3f0b1e7136d949c10a: I do indeed. Something is going on in Darkmeyer that's causing this increased demand. If there's unrest in the city, we might be able to use it to our advantage.
textByHash.3b99c66427a85d707a4cafb50bc489759eeddf6c: Not yet. Your actions will have drawn attention to us. We cannot stay here. It sounds like Damien can do little without Kroy, so I doubt he'll be much trouble anyway.
textByHash.d3fe58f31f77fd207aba3d5a93f2b2c7a81dbdf8: Besides, we've already done too much for these Zamorakians. There are others more deserving of our time.
textByHash.71a72a6cb900ebcb5f510ef4f742ca0c7960c5fa: That's a complicated question friend. But yes, of course we are meant to help people.
textByHash.1da6cc23c384fe95e14230b6d2f8c157e5de4b08: Damien and Carl are small fish in a very big pond. Dealing with them might help the people here, but it will mean nothing to the rest of Morytania.
textByHash.66fd4647639e93252829438a493bb45ee1d0fe83: If we want to help as many people as possible, we need to think bigger than that. The Drakan family, they are the real targets.
textByHash.86b02ad548ca0e94928c23bd6322cfb70a284e34: We've already done a lot to help here. Besides, Damien will get what's coming to him in the end, I'm sure of that.
textByHash.ce5ff8c9a9f14a01ca1bcd176a8030784ac5c9f8: Zamorakians are evil my friend, it does not shame me to say it. But no, if this were a Saradominist town, my actions would be the same.
textByHash.81ab3cb6cbd917feb84bd4c40cce86e0bb4d6c37: You have a good heart my friend. I have a lot of respect for that. I assure you that if we keep on our current path, we will bring freedom to all of Morytania.
textByHash.24b38f5ac75aa68cd432db163515c352285a2e55: Meet me at Paterdomus friend. We will check in with Drezel and Ivan, before continuing our investigation.
textByHash.e526a41eeb19e1aa6b568db85ef958ff951673d4: Be careful out there. Myreque prevail.
textByHash.a98a0003ac3c092ee7de46f29b770fd4543fd4cd: Well met friend. Good to see you made it in one piece.
textByHash.5126b0cca77425466cbd999ccf1ea49eb324c90a: Not yet, I thought it best to wait for you.
textByHash.4ecff4c71531607a9885d63a26e189b0825d9f09: Well Drezel, the reason we are here is due to an investigation we've been conducting in Slepe.
textByHash.a9b161de5258ef70dcd34d11a2cf30531d42c061: Seems that one of their nobles, Damien Leucurte, has been looking for ways to increase blood output. Interestingly, he's been doing it without consent from the Drakan family.
textByHash.89c3d53bb73f2a3ffc808d85ba94191d20865286: I knew it. What's been going on?
textByHash.aa6347403736a86dd4019de703a3c2c7f1305a43: Perhaps their encounter with you scared them off.
textByHash.f686084ab83296757442dfd832d5da0ac2787ed4: Did you send word to the king?
textByHash.ea2e99593f7799b15d4dacd228c7a314df4a1417: Typical. This is why I hate politics.
textByHash.93e2d7256a1e252c60cba08fe4c2e9e89dcb7f29: On the topic of Ivan, where is he Drezel?
textByHash.57761a17ca79543b4c10a999864d5f4de47c30d1: What? You just let him go? He's just a boy!
textByHash.3328409c85806b6552cf21fbbb24f08399e5c3b9: You should have sent word to us! This is for us to deal with, not him!
textByHash.d95aa772b79a2140a2eef16b1ead6b7063c1c8c5: That's not the point! He needs to be kept safe!
textByHash.9b935e14905e43e15cf6f6ad2f0c89d96fe221f8: It's not just about that. There's also the promise I made. Before his mother died, I swore to her on my life that I would shield Ivan from any harm.
textByHash.0c6412422136912cd7a6efc66c8a03c00edb4f97: Though I nearly failed her when Vanstrom killed Sani and Harold.
textByHash.888f39a9659d807790a6ff336d7c8f0b5bdc2b07: And what of his learnings here? You would have him abandon them?
textByHash.e947ff3af33255e5581c1f3eebdc95ea039b1260: Yes, you're absolutely right, friend. Forgive us. Directly south of Fenkenstrain's Castle. Is that right Drezel?
textByHash.6a22c4b2d6d901a23f1134a571422ea7418532cc: Very well. Meet me there. Let's see if we can find out what's going on.
textByHash.2876293a70c1ad8ec1c61bb6152b6986e4305a81: Oh, and , I would appreciate it if you could avoid mentioning the stuff about Ivandis Seergaze to Ivan. One day he will have to bear that burden. But for now, he has enough weight on his shoulders.
textByHash.06ce12db11c6c93f23e1f1a0ba066e12b0eb90f3: Meet me in the Haunted Woods, just south of Fenkenstrain's Castle. We will hopefully find Ivan there.
textByHash.0c0e197d43d50287f675b4cfae24a6368c4e5106: Ivan. It's good to see you safe.
textByHash.f8f2a54378e6af304eb0c14b2cc1714b3889099d: Indeed. But Ivan, you shouldn't be out here. It's not safe for you. Head back to the temple, let and I take it from here.
textByHash.33f661ee0d7ec6eaa0851762fbda088863a5d2ec: So it's as we suspected. The werewolves have been taking people from across the Salve.
textByHash.8e6bb8a07ff15be3df427646817a725f02d36e51: This doesn't give us anything more to go on though. We have our suspicions, but we still don't know for sure why they are doing this.
textByHash.163ffd2494e5f1ed9a44d177adc4ca67eb1b06a2: No, it's too risky. Last thing we need is you getting captured, Ivan.
textByHash.80f63ffd9106da67b120774882c729759f3b711b: And formally, sister to Ranis Drakan, before here came along.
textByHash.5d4408dc4ceb2a71685016099d71608d8036879b: Save your words. You didn't come to thank us for Ranis. Why are you here?
textByHash.ba8177619045c8d59f49168b711ed18f6ada4453: And why would you want that?
textByHash.3cbf6c65574ac9842783783afe9e5a9c76bd901e: Every member of the Myreque? Do you take us for fools? You're trying to lure us all there to wipe us out.
textByHash.76a1e383396e94fb73a089e04c0a3ce2ce44b213: You'll mount our heads above the gates of Castle Drakan. Yeah, we get it.
textByHash.cf243915e91ba33c78ae4433b2d2c08528421455: Yes. But it was with good intention, Ivan. To believe there were more of us out there... It inspired hope in our ranks, and fear in our enemies.
textByHash.eb113a2e9dad3abd3bd8d560c528ca7d64cdf5e8: Better than none at all. Ivan, I have made some tough choices over my years, but every one of them was for the good of Morytania.
textByHash.987143bcbbb1cd57ac17e795c59227b4d2710718: I don't like it either. Later, we will sit down and properly discuss this. However, at the moment I fear we have more pressing matters.
textByHash.8dd83441d011692b75e6a850324d112e1b16b591: Go back to Paterdomus, Ivan. and I will handle it.
textByHash.e222ea0f2017e4621464b91a7f8b75634049c0ad: Come with us? What makes you think we're even going? What makes you think we have any intention of listening to that witch?
textByHash.38720bfca090a8ffd9b7401223b4a2c0c87c7cd7: Dammit Ivan! I'm trying to keep you safe!
textByHash.8fab450ff9fed7502a59a63cd9bccd9058063b5f: Fine. But don't expect me to even pretend to be happy about this! Breaking bread with a vampyre! What have we come to?
textByHash.d4917f3f6ff924e494c8f80fa8e92e0090239772: I will travel down to Burgh de Rott and inform Radigad and Polmafi of this plight we find ourselves in. I'll also have word sent to Safalaan and the others in Meiyerditch.
textByHash.528910b2b99d9e6a1adcf89e4915befb51033b3e: , I would appreciate it if you could escort Ivan down to Burgh de Rott.
textByHash.c51915b96debf84a5d685cd429562b5b93a232d0: I will see you both at the boathouse in Burgh de Rott. We can use the boat there to get to this graveyard. Good luck.
textByHash.995e819069910ee09520cc7ecd91b2125be636c0: Well, here we are. Likely going to our doom.
textByHash.5ceb05d08cc689a10aa2408880464b46a98d669f: Radigad has scouted out the graveyard. It's empty, for now. Vanescula's probably waiting for all of us to arrive before she makes her move.
textByHash.06ab66e141adde9fc620e8450b4c12244aab1310: Polmafi managed to get in contact with them. They will meet us there.
textByHash.4127ba69de70dbd8ed919e45c64b7b5d623f3326: Indeed. No point delaying the inevitable. Hop in the boat once you're ready, friend. We're right behind you.
textByHash.0efaf62e9a9b3d8c4c5c1547f639a3a38d417d98: Hop in the boat once you're ready, friend. We're right behind you.
textByHash.63a65a4bc73717b216eb57edc7dd9fbef213b12e: Good to see you, Safalaan. Though I wish it were under better circumstances.
textByHash.d3546ea91c9cd5e3fb3effe699529614fab6961d: They know we can kill them. They finally know what fear feels like.
textByHash.d7874e755868259228c111ad89668ea91592849b: Strange, and also dangerous. I do not like any aspect of this.
textByHash.9c160a59a573b46c62a8e910e006865819637666: Speaking of , about that other matter I mentioned in my message...
textByHash.73c76b9765537aed58c96d7bb06428a2a51800bf: You've done well, friend. I realise it doesn't mean much, but we wanted to thank you in some way for all of your work so far.
textByHash.bfceab8d390fb1d3b69e36ea511881448ef3bcc1: As ready as we'll ever be. Let's do this.
textByHash.8595c65c6c2784366bd86afe1eb8c580d6f16afd: Well Vanescula, here we are. All the Myreque, just like you wanted.
textByHash.fd3a935d5a4150cfa52e2df1d076ead970409681: Skip the small talk. Why are we here?
textByHash.a35ff730c5540e0dcfa8f88fc9c57402c4eef79c: Yeah, yeah. We've suspected as much for a long time. Why is this relevant?
textByHash.67f2a5afafaaffd2272f8ae15a3478f0812cdc90: So you wish to take his place. What makes you think we'll help you with that?
textByHash.130fee8551d058260190dffc354a1405a88a3680: Too simple, if you ask me.
textByHash.d527d1745ebb63ca28a71c626e147573423db961: Of course I'm not happy. You expect us to just hop into bed with you? You are the enemy! You always have been, and you always will be! We will have no part in this.
textByHash.64641e100616b855ec71729b881c2a1c01a944ae: What makes you think we're just going to let you walk off with him?
textByHash.a40afdfe6f333d1237d52ced00ecef9e53dcea26: Hmm. Well has to go with you. I want him there in case Vanescula here tries anything.
textByHash.c26ebee3e9762642cdfe4519488a5643e2322222: Now's not the time for chat. You're meant to be watching Safalaan and Vanescula.
textByHash.996f3899bacad956e8ff45169c05a28bafc308ae: Well met. Tell me, what's Vanescula up to with Safalaan?
textByHash.66c94ae536f7d9546372de9bf4eb731aa873fc71: Are you sure this isn't a trick? It could be a trap set by Vanescula?
textByHash.c22a80c81a1b2db0c19869440d688f9df99e175f: Hmm. I guess it does fit.
textByHash.e0eb5952ca5eedc690d4566045443844bcbf6de8: Those monsters! How has Safalaan taken this?
textByHash.dbc1560d81dd1ae6c2be633bbd94980e8dd310de: What? This is a bad idea! She is a vampyre, we can't trust her.
textByHash.79fbec984299b0f155fcc2429b202c21f50c7259: No. I'm sorry, but no. She's trying to manipulate Safalaan but I see through her lies. I can't support this.
textByHash.3cc4fb9ac0945cb8adb934934e6a6fbfdf0688b8: There is no more thinking to do. The answer is still no.
textByHash.27dbc3bc896e1c3e8de8e4aac6076166802fe82f: But they must know that we can't trust Vanescula.
textByHash.c29be40453f2c6e37fa62502c16caf6b17a7a460: Damn it. I don't want to work with her, but I have no intention of abandoning the others to their fate. Fine. Looks like we all get to die together.
textByHash.df16f3aa16a28f418e9ed2f35f5ab0dab653f669: Come. I guess we should tell that vampyre that we're in.
textByHash.a1ba01a82b29db192d4a3ba730c827612c23120d: Well Vanescula, it seems you have managed to convince us. Well done.
textByHash.c3ce4d20d3d328b37b44669c2abae268d7c9dd02: It worked well enough on Ranis.
textByHash.4968f6233783c87974bc97b9bd8326779d7b62d3: Keep an eye on Safalaan, won't you? I worry about how he's taking things.
textByHash.efbfb0f2e5cb7d77096aa479821be72ce424222d: Good. Now you should head off to that laboratory. No point wasting time.
textByHash.35a0fb9699cbdbc71fcd0d306bdf9a04c680afe6: ...of your arrogance. What makes you so sure we won't also kill you once your brother is dead?
textByHash.98dd680145f91230231d673490e0b5eef3a21c98: Now this is exactly what I mean.
textByHash.8eecff0e418dd4fbfcbdd17cd152812fbdb2ed25: Hiding behind larger numbers? Not very honourable.
textByHash.b026c971a9238360ec0b7b9b1b69db9fa5c3988a: Ah, , you're back. Good to see you made it okay.
textByHash.f664b8007bd18bc0dff29608c7b65bf63f00465b: I see where we're going with this. You want us to use this tree to construct new weapons?
textByHash.0ef80930f6d1507b6620f4e9a5d8a084503119f2: Perfect for killing your brother. Assuming you don't kill us first.
textByHash.a7dcd645af4bb17954726292942e4550f9490a4a: I'll believe it when I see it.
textByHash.00e91a916ae63a9aa09a68e6db6b8ab9de137406: I don't see why we'd need to. Vanescula here can get some of this wood for us.
textByHash.fbca55ea38abf73fc1b924eec3dc5aec56fb7439: Polmafi knows how to use a needle. He should be able to sort us out.
textByHash.a6c269d7470a1dc9b6318bc5da4562f613f126b4: You should get going friend. That disguise won't sort itself out.
textByHash.c17d746e6193921cf275314410b5f2d4a7eff300: Of course. Go on then, give us some of your blood.
textByHash.97adec9279d430c6fe9d03d350a60e788025e82b: Ah yes, how silly of me to not realise. You clearly have a vampyre in mind. Spit it out.
textByHash.2c84aa2a3c5a8bfb4f39c08857e3e0551d81c656: Indeed. Let's get it done. , meet me outside the church in Slepe. We're going to pay Damien a little visit.
textByHash.63319d10c3a66e6326dacf43ea30ef1d0d13f046: Meet me outside the church in Slepe. We're going to pay Damien a little visit.
textByHash.ec939eed3d94d2a1368a4ffefbe5a837c30dac7e: Well met friend. Didn't think we'd be back here so soon.
textByHash.ae5226e45d4debcdaa8621f24335db521a280cd8: I suppose you're right. Now, I've done some scouting. It seems Damien is hiding in the manor. Interestingly, he doesn't seem to have increased his security since Kroy's death.
textByHash.7dbc3cdcd03b8943a9bb58ac49031ec86d5072d5: They don't want to mess with you my friend. I suspect you're becoming quite notorious amongst the vampyres.
textByHash.2dbd455405f339e5da3b091bd12b7b891da02f3c: Looks like it. I think we can get in through the dungeon. Let me know when you're ready and we'll head on in.
textByHash.eedc812d7496a284218b183c4b0b753de961b12c: Well you'd better go and get it. I'll be here when you are ready.
textByHash.b93660fcb67a55376834748307c5c849fe86a749: You tell me. You've spent more time with him since his discovery.
textByHash.8d47b4dca20343ba011e43841fd6371dec8054b7: Maintaining a good sense of humour is common coping mechanism when dealing with shock. I hope he's not just putting on a facade. If he's struggling, he should seek our support.
textByHash.9b718933294c4e05ea8132a63bd4b4ec2d45c9b0: It's good that he has people like you. I may have known him a long time, but I can't pretend to be any good at comforting people in such situations. I never manage to say the right thing.
textByHash.08e289f2ba4e096dcb5104847d5c70c4a2f53f51: You can't be any worse than me. I'd take that as a victory.
textByHash.5049fa73655d2b014f2b541699daf744c3882ffc: I don't know. He may well soon come face to face with Lowerniel Drakan, the cause of all his pain. I hope he is ready for that.
textByHash.17b957f4f702a1bde572336430f240646dcbf6f1: And then there's Vanstrom, of course. Their paths are likely to cross again before all this is over. And when that happens, who knows what the outcome will be.
textByHash.5026d1ffa95fc058b7468d7ded313a6a76e5bd35: Anyway, are you ready to deal with Damien?
textByHash.a95b34d9b1eb3d504c5f8ccba02f2a33a9d3c99d: Well, she hasn't killed us yet, which I consider a win. It seems she spoke the truth about wanting her brother dead.
textByHash.0ec89965e500354d57e77c2ea8ab19d090787e7f: Of course, I still fully expect her to betray us once he's gone.
textByHash.f90b6264f2d406bcb4c710a46ebf266d4bc7f102: I wouldn't say I'm 'willing' as such, but what other choice do I have? The others want to do it and I'll be damned if I'm just going to leave them to their fates.
textByHash.2ea694faa8cc9cdf26ac0a22dc4f834a9fd9f115: That doesn't mean I haven't been considering contingencies, of course. I've asked Ivan to prepare us a teleport spell in case we need to make a hasty exit.
textByHash.084a02840027fd62214cd6cd22d721147454ec31: Indeed. But enough about Vanescula. Are you ready to deal with Damien?
textByHash.f34107f613ab43b9cca07d27508d57e24d27ebdd: Are you ready to deal with Damien?
textByHash.ba054c6f3f0f427d94273a28dd99fa81793aef08: We'd hate to disappoint. It's over Damien.
textByHash.b410ed20cfd8d18b3c16362550dad1c50f67c066: Once, yes. But now, we are not the ones cowering.
textByHash.fee488df3f4f2e08e072558b4a53a2c891c422c5: Face it Damien, you're out of time. There's nowhere to run.
textByHash.fc97a0e319449fbeacb8c5d428b1d85750748169: Good. I don't want to stick around this place any longer than needed. I will meet you back at the graveyard.
textByHash.979e05e5ac9a3d1a5d5a19d14116a0db53061167: The death of Damien can't do any harm. That said, don't expect this to suddenly fix everything around here. The Sleeper Plague isn't the only illness in this town.
textByHash.656df6444702672d251e23d3a0b49adb627a0c58: I do. The people here chose to follow Zamorak. A choice like that has consequences. Bad ones.
textByHash.8e3ac408ae33a06d68183556bfb7cdbf4beb7e11: Not exactly. I don't believe in fate myself. I'm sure someone like Drezel would say otherwise, though. I just think that if you play with fire, you're going to end up getting burnt.
textByHash.896de7a69e7671d8e11bdd0839fcf60388454ebc: Well there shouldn't be any new cases now that Damien and Kroy are gone. That won't help those already infected though.
textByHash.cca0358a9acc95e35edcbd550883a5d080c9a053: Maybe. I suspect this town holds a lot of secrets... but now is not the time to explore them. We have other business to attend to.
textByHash.9a62fb712ee7d83f8c4bfd4643e6697587833c10: No need for us to stick around this place any more. I will meet you back at the graveyard.
textByHash.b7b4d9ffcdb1a77d3de90ad937b8571fd8307263: I sent some of Damien's blood to Polmafi. Speak to him if you need a replacement disguise.
textByHash.82f2c5c22963c1e46aefc25bde530911b694904c: Seems suspiciously convenient. Why hasn't the crack been filled in?
textByHash.01b62f113b9f1e97f91c424ba45f9d12d0949608: You should get going friend. Darkmeyer awaits.
textByHash.4bf8f9e8638f3d21cb2f115d6ea528a65f5081df: Good work . Did it all go okay?
textByHash.75206db8196eb69bc3861174d5daa692337d1c00: He said he needed a walk. I told him not to, but he insisted he'd be fine.
textByHash.b75371ef0d1238f839c01c180026ac1f1c5c5d70: He's at the Meiyerditch hideout. Make sure to take the logs with you. The others need them for their own weapons.
textByHash.8e80c8c68f8f2bf26e575375dde9b6e16f066000: Deliver those blisterwood logs to Vertida in the Meiyerditch hideout. He'll show you how to make the new weapons out of them.
textByHash.1d30938f11b04e313a8186a7a12b5e576357158d: Well met. Do you have your weapon?
textByHash.93acf5e3ef1db115479dccf044fe7ad425f5a787: Well met. I'm guessing you have your weapon?
textByHash.8c77f2bdbe597ff1d6c74831e1daa8d642a4360e: And you took the logs to the others?
textByHash.4c108427fb13499547ab2c4a556c1c0955df371a: There's a path just north of the graveyard. He went for a walk in that direction.
textByHash.545710b21fe472bb19e7d46fca62b2e3b1997ca6: You need to try and find Safalaan. He went for a walk just north of the graveyard.
textByHash.cd342638f671ec4a236a7ec8151977efc46500eb: We need to get back to Darkmeyer.
textByHash.f30ecbba6b27e26dd11e012a85e11ef493fe57a4: Luckily for you, I had another made. Although I'll have to charge you for it.
textByHash.dfcbd1d5814dca478d1c56e671f3d5484a6ed5d9: I'll be here when you do.
textByHash.9e7d6de8e7e952fcd0d5017222f93ba9690bd8aa: Here you go. Now, are you ready?
textByHash.5b01075dbbaca4f50654a43e9c84fe28e65727f0: I've managed to retrieve some of your things. I imagine you'll want them back.
textByHash.52de6d45f1fb74d4b85587f464814f6c5ea5cdf3: I don't have anything for you friend. If I did have any of your items, but you died before collecting them, they'll now be lost.
textByHash.a87c012b6cfbd87463c53b453534e925d3e33ef7: Ivan, teleport him out of there!
textByHash.00fad510382eb8e4f65e6c653e420ab14ca9d047: , get over here! We need to go. Now!
textByHash.56a1acfa849d28dafdc63ce57478cbab733252b6: What do we do? There's nothing we can do. Don't you see, Ivan? It's over. They've won.
